question: He had no idea why the computer broke. He felt completely __________.
What __________ you usually do on Friday evenings after school?
He __________ the solution to the puzzle after thinking about it for hours.
The government used television broadcasts to __________ the emergency message nationwide.
Lola __________ it before she discovered a snake at her feet and screamed.
We hadn't met before today, __________?
Let's go to the cinema this evening, __________?
Everyone enjoyed the party last night, __________ they?
Young people __________ by hand less and less often in recent years.
Parents can learn __________ ways to limit their children's internet use.
Read: 'The invention of the telegraph in 1837 sped up communication dramatically.' — In what year was the telegraph invented?
Read: 'Since the dawn of time, people have been using different ways of communicating at a distance.' — Which tense is used in this sentence?
Read: 'Jane spelled out to the scientific community for the first time that animals, too, could use tools.' — The phrasal verb 'spelled out' means:
Our boss is completely fed up with all the paperwork, __________?
I __________ been a teacher since 2002 — it's been a wonderful career.
He kept asking one question after another — he was __________ for ten minutes.
Nothing ever seems to change in this situation, __________ it?
Read: 'her calmness gained their trust. It was only later that Jane would learn how dangerous they could be.' — What does this suggest about Jane?
choices: She was a trained animal expert
She knew they were dangerous from the start
She was brave but unaware of the danger at first

question: 'Who would have __________ it?' — Complete the expression of surprise.
The kids __________ in the garden when it suddenly started to rain heavily.
Nothing is changing and he is becoming more and more __________ about the situation.
Read: 'At this time, people believed that only humans could use tools, but Jane watched chimpanzees using sticks to take ants from a tree.' — What did Jane prove for the first time?
choices: Chimpanzees are more intelligent than humans
Animals can also use tools, not only humans

question: Which phrase best expresses ANNOYANCE with a situation?
choices: This is driving me up the wall!

question: The instructions __________ exactly what to do in case of an emergency.
The crowd went __________ when their team scored the winning goal in extra time.
I __________ already __________ to that officer, so he knew I was innocent.
Read: 'India and China are considered to be the main emerging economies.' — The word 'emerging' in this context means:
By the time we arrived at the airport, my father already __________ left.
While I __________ TV, my phone fell off the table and broke.
She is __________ about her trip to Japan — she has been counting the days.
Don't __________ in front of everyone — ask her the difficult question in private.
Mozart __________ more than 600 pieces of music in his short life.
'__________ a weight off my mind!' — Complete the expression of relief.
Don't forget to call me when you arrive safely, __________?
The phone __________ every five minutes today — this is very annoying!
What __________ you do last weekend? Did you go anywhere special?
He can swim very well, __________?
Read: 'This was attributed to a mixture of cultural reasons and past government policies.' — Why can only 25% of UK adults speak a foreign language?
choices: Because English is the only language needed
A mixture of cultural reasons and past government policies
Because all UK adults speak Latin instead
Because foreign language teaching is illegal

question: Read the whole essay 'Regulated AI is Good for Children'. What is the writer's MAIN ARGUMENT?
choices: AI is always beneficial for children and society
Children should never use any form of technology
AI can be good for children, but only if it is carefully regulated and controlled
AI should be banned for all children immediately

question: The reporter used smoke signals to __________ the message to the next village.
